claim: Gemini 2.5 native audio out allows style control via prompts: whisper, laugh, speak angrily; temperature controls stochasticity
actionable: Use prompt-based style descriptions for TTS; test different prompt formulations to reduce overacting

claim: Multi-speaker TTS with Gemini can recreate podcast-style dialogue with interruptions, laughter, natural turn-taking
actionable: Use Gemini TTS for podcast generation; script multi-speaker interactions and provide speaker voice/style configuration

claim: YAML frontmatter in skill.md is always loaded; full skill.md is read on-demand when agent decides to use the skill
actionable: Structure TTS service integrations as skills with concise frontmatter and detailed implementation examples

claim: Gemini 2.5 Flash TTS model produces good quality voices; 2.5 Pro may be better for complex emotions and multi-speaker
actionable: Start with Flash model for cost; switch to Pro if multi-speaker or emotional nuance is critical

claim: Sample rate is 24K; token pricing not yet announced (preview); cost-effectiveness vs open models for real-time use unknown
actionable: Monitor Gemini TTS pricing when released; compare with Kakoro and other open models for real-time latency requirements

claim: Open TTS models (Kakoro) preferable for local/real-time use; cloud Gemini TTS has latency but flexible control
actionable: Choose local open models for latency-critical apps; use cloud APIs for batch audio generation and podcast workflows
